propositions: Repoint the global signing.sign_manifest seam so all producers mint rebar.opcert.v1 Ed25519 DSSE op-certs instead of legacy per-clone HMAC signatures, yielding one signature per verdict.

First signing use auto-generates a passphrase-free Ed25519 keypair at <tracker>/.opcert-key (mode 0600, git-ignored) via a race-safe exclusive-create mechanism (0700 staging dir, os.link commit point, derivative .pub), and verify-side never creates a key.

Expand-phase read path dispatches by record shape: envelope-bearing records verify against the environment's own public key via the op-cert scheme, while legacy signature-bearing records route unchanged to the HMAC verify_record path.

Drift-refresh and resign re-sign as op-certs so a refresh never downgrades a certified op-cert back to an HMAC record; close-gate producer rides the same seam with its never-sign and material-drift guards preserved.

Output schema contract is updated across sign_result.schema.json, SignResultOut, and SignResult TypedDict to admit both envelope and legacy HMAC shapes, and verify_signature_result verdict enum is widened with invalid/unavailable/unknown_kind/unknown_scheme.

If ssh-keygen is unavailable or the key is unreadable and cannot be regenerated, signing records an in-band {signed:false} outcome without blocking local operations, and gates that require the signature block with an OpenSSH remediation message.
